articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2047' Blueprint for Developed India, Focus on Green Growth & Digital Economy
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister's Office releases comprehensive framework for 'Viksit Bharat @2047'.
• Emphasizes sustainable development, technological innovation, and inclusive growth.
• Key pillars include green energy transition, digital public infrastructure expansion, and human capital development.
• Calls for collaborative efforts from states, private sector, and civil society.
• Aims to position India as a global economic powerhouse by its centenary of independence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047 aims for India to be a developed nation by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
Focus areas include green growth, digital economy, human capital development.
The vision is based on principles of 'Panch Pran' enunciated by the Prime Minister.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in achieving Viksit Bharat @2047 targets, including social inequalities, environmental concerns, and geopolitical uncertainties.
Analyze the crucial role of green growth and digital public infrastructure (DPI) in fostering sustainable and inclusive development.
Evaluate the necessity of a multi-stakeholder approach, involving central and state governments, private sector, and civil society, for realizing the national vision.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing 'Viksit Bharat @2047' logo with elements of green energy and digital connectivity.
articles.trivia: India aims to be a developed nation by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.

articles.headline: RBI Raises Concerns Over Global Economic Slowdown, Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate
articles.explainer:
• Reserve Bank of India's latest monetary policy review highlights risks from global slowdown and geopolitical tensions.
• Cites persistent inflationary pressures, albeit moderating, as a key factor in policy stance.
• Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decides to keep the repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive time.
• Focus remains on aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Emphasizes vigilance on liquidity management and financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC sets the repo rate, which is the rate at which commercial banks borrow money from the RBI.
The current inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
The MPC comprises six members, three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and growth promotion in a volatile global economic environment.
Discuss the impact of a global economic slowdown and geopolitical tensions on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools, such as the repo rate and liquidity management, in achieving their objectives.
articles.imageDescription: RBI building with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members, three from RBI and three appointed by the government.

articles.headline: India Calls for Strengthened Multilateralism at UN Security Council Debate
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN addresses the UNSC on the need for comprehensive UN reforms.
• Stresses the importance of equitable representation and inclusive decision-making in global governance.
• Highlights challenges posed by rising protectionism, climate change, and persistent terrorism.
• Reiterates India's commitment to multilateral solutions to global problems.
• Emphasizes the urgency of Security Council expansion to reflect contemporary realities.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: The UN Security Council (UNSC) has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member of the UNSC.
The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent UNSC seats for themselves.
mainsPoints: Examine India's arguments for UNSC reforms and its implications for global power dynamics and the principle of 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam'.
Discuss the challenges to effective multilateralism in the 21st century, including geopolitical rivalries, protectionism, and the rise of non-state actors.
Evaluate India's role in promoting a rules-based international order and its vision for a reformed United Nations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian flag alongside the UN logo.
articles.trivia: India has served eight terms as a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council.

articles.headline: New National Clean Air Programme Targets for 2030 Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announces revised targets for NCAP.
• Aims for 50-60% reduction in particulate matter concentration in 131 non-attainment cities by 2030.
• Focus on source apportionment studies, real-time monitoring, and technological interventions.
• Enhanced funding and inter-ministerial coordination for effective implementation.
• Emphasizes public awareness and behavioral change for better air quality management.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019.
The original target of NCAP was to achieve a 20-30% reduction in PM concentration by 2024.
Non-attainment cities are those that consistently fail to meet national ambient air quality standards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of NCAP in improving urban air quality since its inception and the reasons for revising targets.
Discuss the socio-economic and health impacts of air pollution in India, particularly on vulnerable populations.
Suggest innovative and sustainable solutions for achieving ambitious clean air targets, including technological, policy, and behavioral changes.
articles.imageDescription: Image of Delhi skyline with reduced smog, symbolizing cleaner air.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, air pollution is one of the biggest environmental threats to human health.

articles.headline: ISRO Prepares for Venus Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' Launch in Early 2025
articles.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) confirms readiness for its maiden mission to Venus.
• Shukrayaan-1 will study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface using various instruments.
• Mission aims to understand the planet's geological evolution and atmospheric composition.
• Collaboration with international partners for some payloads and data analysis.
• Represents India's expanding capabilities in interplanetary exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first dedicated mission to Venus.
Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass.
ISRO's previous interplanetary missions include Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) and Chandrayaan series to the Moon.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's planetary exploration missions in enhancing its scientific prowess and technological capabilities.
Analyze the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of Shukrayaan-1, particularly regarding Venus's extreme environment and geological activity.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in space missions and how it benefits India's space program.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter around Venus.
articles.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in our solar system, meaning the sun rises in the west and sets in the east.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Public Grievance Redressal Mechanisms
articles.explainer:
• Apex court issues strong directives to state governments on timely resolution of public complaints.
• Calls for robust, transparent, and accessible grievance redressal systems, including digital platforms.
• Emphasizes accountability of public officials in addressing citizen issues.
• Notes backlog of grievances hindering effective governance and citizen trust.
• Seeks compliance reports from Chief Secretaries within three months.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Citizen charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court is the highest judicial body in India, with powers to issue directives to states.
E-governance initiatives aim to improve service delivery, transparency, and efficiency in public administration.
Article 21 of the Constitution protects the right to life and personal liberty, which implicitly includes the right to good governance and timely redressal of grievances.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of effective public grievance redressal for ensuring good governance, promoting citizen trust, and upholding the rule of law.
Analyze the challenges faced by state governments in implementing robust and accessible Public Grievance Redressal Systems (PGRS), including bureaucratic inertia and lack of resources.
Suggest measures to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen participation in public administration through effective grievance mechanisms and digital platforms.
articles.imageDescription: Scales of justice overlaid with a digital interface.
articles.trivia: The Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) is a common platform for citizens to lodge grievances against government organizations.

articles.headline: India's Logistics Performance Index (LPI) Shows Improvement, Challenges Remain
articles.explainer:
• World Bank's latest LPI report indicates India's continued progress in logistics infrastructure and services.
• Improvements noted in customs efficiency, infrastructure quality, and international shipments.
• Digitalization of logistics processes and multi-modal connectivity projects contribute to the rise.
• Challenges persist in cost efficiency, lead times, and achieving last-mile connectivity.
• Government's National Logistics Policy (NLP) aims to further streamline the sector.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: The Logistics Performance Index (LPI) is an interactive benchmarking tool created by the World Bank.
It evaluates six key dimensions of logistics performance: customs, infrastructure, international shipments, logistics competence, tracking & tracing, and timeliness.
The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan aims for integrated planning and synchronized implementation of infrastructure projects.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of a robust and efficient logistics sector for India's economic growth, global competitiveness, and 'Make in India' initiative.
Discuss the key initiatives undertaken by the government under the National Logistics Policy (NLP) to streamline the sector and reduce logistics costs.
Identify the remaining challenges in improving India's LPI ranking, particularly in areas like cost efficiency, lead times, and last-mile connectivity.
articles.imageDescription: Container ships at a port with trucks and trains, symbolizing multi-modal logistics.
articles.trivia: India aims to reduce the cost of logistics as a percentage of GDP from 13-14% to 8% by 2030.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Combat Malnutrition in Tribal Regions
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Health announce a joint initiative.
• Focuses on improving nutritional outcomes for women and children in remote tribal areas.
• Includes provision of fortified food, health check-ups, and awareness campaigns.
• Leverages local community health workers (ASHAs, Anganwadi) for last-mile delivery.
• Aims to address the specific dietary and health challenges faced by tribal populations.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Malnutrition, including stunting, wasting, and anemia, is a significant public health issue in India.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) is the government's flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Tribal populations often face higher rates of malnutrition due to geographical isolation, food insecurity, and limited access to healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges, including socio-economic, environmental, and cultural factors, that contribute to malnutrition in tribal communities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government interventions and welfare schemes in addressing tribal health disparities and nutritional deficiencies.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and community-led approaches for sustainable nutritional security and improved health outcomes in these regions.
articles.imageDescription: A community health worker interacting with tribal women and children.
articles.trivia: India has the largest tribal population in the world, with over 700 recognized Scheduled Tribes.

articles.headline: India and France Conduct Joint Naval Exercise in Arabian Sea
articles.explainer:
• Indian Navy and French Navy conclude a three-day bilateral exercise 'Varuna' in the Arabian Sea.
• Focus on interoperability, maritime security operations, and anti-submarine warfare.
• Involved frontline warships, submarines, and maritime patrol aircraft from both navies.
• Underscores growing strategic partnership and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Aims to enhance mutual understanding and operational capabilities against common threats.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: 'Varuna' is a prominent bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
France is a key strategic partner for India, particularly in defense, space, and civil nuclear energy sectors.
The Arabian Sea is part of the Indian Ocean, which is a critical maritime trade route and strategically important region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-France defense cooperation in the context of Indo-Pacific security and counterbalancing regional challenges.
Discuss how joint naval exercises like 'Varuna' enhance maritime domain awareness, interoperability, and regional stability.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in bilateral defense partnerships, including technology transfer, joint development, and regional security architectures.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French warships sailing in formation at sea.
articles.trivia: India and France also conduct joint air exercise 'Garuda' and joint army exercise 'Shakti'.

articles.headline: Experts Call for Stronger Measures to Protect Western Ghats Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• Environmentalists and scientists highlight escalating threats to the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Concerns raised over unchecked development, deforestation, and climate change impacts.
• Advocate for stricter implementation of environmental regulations and buffer zone protection.
• Emphasize the unique ecological significance of the region as a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Calls for integrated management plans involving all six states sharing the Ghats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and its conservation
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and are spread across six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
The Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees provided recommendations for the conservation and protection of the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats, including its role as a water tower and repository of biodiversity, and the various threats it currently faces.
Analyze the recommendations of expert committees like Gadgil and Kasturirangan for Western Ghats conservation and the challenges in their implementation.
Propose a sustainable development model for the region that balances conservation needs with the livelihood requirements of local communities.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a waterfall.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: India Develops Indigenous AI Tool for Early Detection of Oral Cancer
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at IIT Delhi, in collaboration with AIIMS, unveil a groundbreaking AI-powered diagnostic tool.
• Utilizes machine learning algorithms to analyze medical images for early signs of oral cancer.
• Aims to improve diagnosis accuracy and reduce diagnostic delays, especially in rural areas.
• Undergoing clinical trials and seeking regulatory approvals for widespread use.
• Highlights India's growing prowess in applying AI for public health challenges.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science focused on creating intelligent machines.
Oral cancer is a major health concern in India, often linked to tobacco use, and early detection significantly improves treatment outcomes.
Indigenous development of AI tools contributes to self-reliance in critical sectors like healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing healthcare delivery, diagnostics, and treatment in India.
Analyze the ethical considerations, data privacy concerns, and challenges in deploying AI-based diagnostic tools in a diverse healthcare landscape.
Evaluate how indigenous AI solutions can effectively address specific Indian health challenges and contribute to equitable access to healthcare.
articles.imageDescription: Medical professional using a tablet with an AI interface to analyze oral scans.
articles.trivia: India accounts for nearly one-third of the global burden of oral cancer.

articles.headline: Centre-State Dialogue on Fiscal Decentralization for Local Bodies
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Finance convenes a meeting with state representatives to discuss fiscal transfers to Panchayats and Municipalities.
• Focus on strengthening the financial autonomy of local self-governments.
• Deliberations on recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission regarding local body grants.
• Aims to ensure effective devolution of funds for grassroots development.
• Emphasizes the need for robust accounting and auditing mechanisms at the local level.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Finance Commission
prelimsPoints: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments institutionalized Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India.
The Finance Commission (Article 280) recommends the distribution of taxes between the Union and States, and grants-in-aid to states.
State Finance Commissions recommend the principles for the distribution of resources to local bodies within a state.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by local self-governments in India regarding financial autonomy and their impact on grassroots development.
Analyze the role of the Union Finance Commission in strengthening fiscal federalism and promoting devolution of funds to the local level.
Suggest measures to enhance the financial viability, accountability, and capacity building of Panchayats and Municipalities for effective governance.
articles.imageDescription: Diagram illustrating flow of funds from central government to states and local bodies.
articles.trivia: The 16th Finance Commission was constituted in December 2023 and is expected to submit its report by October 2025.

articles.headline: Remembering Guru Gobind Singh on His Birth Anniversary
articles.explainer:
• Nation commemorates the birth anniversary of Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Sikh Guru.
• Celebrated as Prakash Parv, marking his profound contributions to Sikhism and Indian society.
• His teachings emphasize equality, courage, and selfless service.
• Known for establishing the Khalsa Panth and compiling the Guru Granth Sahib.
• His life and philosophy continue to inspire millions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Bhakti movement
prelimsPoints: Guru Gobind Singh was the 10th and last human Guru of Sikhs.
He established the Khalsa Panth, a collective body of initiated Sikhs, in 1699.
He declared the Guru Granth Sahib as the eternal and living Guru of Sikhs.
His birth anniversary is celebrated as Prakash Parv by Sikhs worldwide.
articles.trivia: Guru Gobind Singh introduced the five 'K's (Kesh, Kangha, Kara, Kirpan, Kacchera) that Khalsa Sikhs are required to wear.

articles.headline: Enhanced Focus on Coastal Security after Recent Incidents
articles.explainer:
• Indian Coast Guard and Navy intensify surveillance along coastal areas following intelligence inputs.
• Review of existing coastal security framework involving state police and customs.
• Emphasis on integrating technology, including radar systems and drones, for real-time monitoring.
• Training and capacity building for local fishing communities as 'eyes and ears' of security agencies.
• Aims to prevent infiltration, smuggling, and other illicit activities from the sea.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is responsible for maritime law enforcement and coastal security in India's territorial waters.
The 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks highlighted significant vulnerabilities in India's coastal security framework.
The Multi-Agency Centre (MAC) serves as a common platform for intelligence sharing among various security agencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional threats, including terrorism, smuggling, and illegal migration, to India's extensive coastline.
Analyze the measures taken by the government to strengthen coastal defense mechanisms and enhance maritime domain awareness since major security incidents.
Evaluate the role of technology, inter-agency coordination, and community participation in ensuring comprehensive coastal security.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard ship patrolling the coastline.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including island territories, making coastal security a complex challenge.

articles.headline: Government Promotes Climate-Resilient Agriculture Through Digital Tools
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of Agriculture launches new initiatives to integrate digital technologies for climate-smart farming.
• Focus on precision agriculture, weather advisories, and market linkages through mobile apps.
• Aims to help farmers adapt to changing climate patterns and enhance crop productivity.
• Provides subsidies for adoption of climate-resilient crop varieties and water-efficient irrigation.
• Part of broader efforts to double farmers' income and ensure food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: Climate-resilient agriculture aims to reduce the vulnerability of agriculture to the adverse impacts of climate change.
Digital tools like remote sensing, AI, and IoT are being increasingly used for precision farming and providing real-time weather advisories.
India's agriculture sector is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts, including erratic rainfall and extreme weather events.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and farmers' livelihoods.
Analyze how digital technologies and precision farming can enhance climate resilience, optimize resource utilization, and improve productivity in the agricultural sector.
Evaluate the policy interventions and support mechanisms required to promote sustainable and climate-smart agriculture among small and marginal farmers.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer using a smartphone in a field with modern irrigation systems.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top agricultural producers globally, but over 50% of its cultivable land is rainfed, making it susceptible to monsoon variations.

highlights: Government unveils 'Viksit Bharat @2047' blueprint focusing on green growth and digital economy.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate amidst global economic slowdown concerns, reiterates focus on inflation.
India calls for comprehensive UN Security Council reforms, advocating for equitable representation and inclusive global governance.
New National Clean Air Programme targets announced, aiming for 50-60% PM reduction by 2030 in non-attainment cities.
ISRO gears up for its maiden Venus mission, 'Shukrayaan-1', slated for an early 2025 launch.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Viksit Bharat @2047' vision:
1. It aims for India to become a developed nation by the 100th year of its independence.
2. Green energy transition and expansion of digital public infrastructure are key pillars.
3. The vision exclusively focuses on economic growth, sidelining social development.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The 'Viksit Bharat @2047' vision aims for India to be a developed nation by 2047, the centenary of its independence. Statement 2 is correct: Key pillars include green energy transition, digital public infrastructure expansion, and human capital development. Statement 3 is incorrect: The vision emphasizes inclusive growth and social development, not sidelining them.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), consider the following statements:
1. It was launched in 2019 by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
2. The original target was to reduce particulate matter concentration by 50-60% by 2024.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: NCAP was launched in 2019 by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Statement 2 is incorrect: The original target was to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024. The 50-60% reduction is the *new* target set for 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Western Ghats is/are correct?
1. It is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
2. It is one of the world's eight "hottest hotspots" of biological diversity.
3. It extends across five Indian states only.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Western Ghats is indeed a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity. Statement 3 is incorrect; it extends across six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Varuna' exercise is a bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and which of the following countries?
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Varuna' is the bilateral naval exercise conducted between India and France. Other notable bilateral exercises include Malabar (Quad nations), Indra (India-Russia), and Dharma Guardian (India-Japan Army).

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Finance Commission in India, consider the following statements:
1. It is a constitutional body established under Article 280 of the Constitution.
2. It recommends the distribution of taxes between the Union and the States.
3. It also recommends the principles governing grants-in-aid to the Panchayats and Municipalities directly.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Finance Commission is a constitutional body under Article 280 and recommends the distribution of taxes and grants-in-aid *to the states*. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Union Finance Commission recommends grants-in-aid to states, which *then* devolve funds to local bodies based on recommendations of the *State Finance Commissions*. The Union Finance Commission's mandate is primarily for Union-State fiscal relations.

mainsMocks.question: "Achieving the 'Viksit Bharat @2047' vision requires a multi-pronged strategy encompassing economic growth, social progress, and environmental sustainability." Discuss this statement in light of India's current developmental trajectory and the challenges ahead.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Government Budgeting; Investment models. GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Development processes and the development industry.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Viksit Bharat @2047' as a national aspiration. Elaborate on the multi-pronged strategy: economic (GDP growth, manufacturing, digital economy), social (human capital, health, education, poverty reduction), and environmental (green energy, climate resilience, sustainable resource use). Discuss current trajectory (e.g., fast-growing economy, digital penetration) and significant challenges (e.g., inequality, climate change vulnerability, geopolitical risks, infrastructure gaps). Conclude with the need for strong governance, policy coherence, and public participation.

mainsMocks.question: India's persistent call for reforms in the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) reflects its growing global aspirations and the changing geopolitical realities. Critically analyze India's arguments for a permanent seat and the obstacles it faces.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating India's consistent advocacy for UNSC reforms. Present India's arguments for a permanent seat: its status as the world's largest democracy, a major economy, significant contributor to UN peacekeeping, and its demographic size. Explain how the current UNSC structure is anachronistic. Discuss the obstacles: resistance from existing P5 members (especially on veto power), lack of consensus among UN member states (e.g., Uniting for Consensus group), and the complex nature of the reform process. Conclude with the implications of an unreformed UNSC for global governance and India's foreign policy.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant policy interventions, malnutrition remains a critical public health challenge in India, particularly among vulnerable sections like tribal communities. Examine the underlying causes of malnutrition in tribal regions and suggest comprehensive strategies for its eradication.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections; Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Define malnutrition and its prevalence in India, specifically highlighting the tribal context. Analyze underlying causes: geographical isolation, limited access to healthcare and nutritious food, poor sanitation, traditional dietary practices, lack of awareness, poverty, and socio-cultural barriers. Discuss existing government interventions (e.g., Poshan Abhiyaan, ICDS). Suggest comprehensive strategies: multi-sectoral approach (health, agriculture, WASH, education), culturally sensitive interventions, strengthening public distribution system, promoting diversified farming, empowering women, leveraging technology for monitoring, and improving access to primary healthcare. Conclude with the importance of a holistic and community-led approach.

quickBytes.text: 'Viksit Bharat @2047' aims for India to become a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate).
The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent seats on the UN Security Council.
The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019 to reduce particulate matter concentration.
ISRO's maiden mission to Venus is named 'Shukrayaan-1', aiming to study its atmosphere and surface.
The Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) is a platform for citizen grievances.
The Logistics Performance Index (LPI) is an interactive benchmarking tool created by the World Bank.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight "hottest hotspots" of biological diversity.
Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Sikh Guru, established the Khalsa Panth in 1699.
India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, critical for its trade and security.

GS1.headline: Remembering Guru Gobind Singh on His Birth Anniversary
GS1.explainer:
• Nation commemorates the birth anniversary of Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Sikh Guru.
• Celebrated as Prakash Parv, marking his profound contributions to Sikhism and Indian society.
• His teachings emphasize equality, courage, and selfless service.
• Known for establishing the Khalsa Panth and compiling the Guru Granth Sahib.
• His life and philosophy continue to inspire millions.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Bhakti movement
prelimsPoints: Guru Gobind Singh was the 10th and last human Guru of Sikhs.
He established the Khalsa Panth, a collective body of initiated Sikhs, in 1699.
He declared the Guru Granth Sahib as the eternal and living Guru of Sikhs.
His birth anniversary is celebrated as Prakash Parv by Sikhs worldwide.
GS1.trivia: Guru Gobind Singh introduced the five 'K's (Kesh, Kangha, Kara, Kirpan, Kacchera) that Khalsa Sikhs are required to wear.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Strengthened Multilateralism at UN Security Council Debate
GS2.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the UN addresses the UNSC on the need for comprehensive UN reforms.
• Stresses the importance of equitable representation and inclusive decision-making in global governance.
• Highlights challenges posed by rising protectionism, climate change, and persistent terrorism.
• Reiterates India's commitment to multilateral solutions to global problems.
• Emphasizes the urgency of Security Council expansion to reflect contemporary realities.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: The UN Security Council (UNSC) has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member of the UNSC.
The G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent UNSC seats for themselves.
mainsPoints: Examine India's arguments for UNSC reforms and its implications for global power dynamics and the principle of 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam'.
Discuss the challenges to effective multilateralism in the 21st century, including geopolitical rivalries, protectionism, and the rise of non-state actors.
Evaluate India's role in promoting a rules-based international order and its vision for a reformed United Nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian flag alongside the UN logo.
GS2.trivia: India has served eight terms as a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Public Grievance Redressal Mechanisms
GS2.explainer:
• Apex court issues strong directives to state governments on timely resolution of public complaints.
• Calls for robust, transparent, and accessible grievance redressal systems, including digital platforms.
• Emphasizes accountability of public officials in addressing citizen issues.
• Notes backlog of grievances hindering effective governance and citizen trust.
• Seeks compliance reports from Chief Secretaries within three months.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
Citizen charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court is the highest judicial body in India, with powers to issue directives to states.
E-governance initiatives aim to improve service delivery, transparency, and efficiency in public administration.
Article 21 of the Constitution protects the right to life and personal liberty, which implicitly includes the right to good governance and timely redressal of grievances.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of effective public grievance redressal for ensuring good governance, promoting citizen trust, and upholding the rule of law.
Analyze the challenges faced by state governments in implementing robust and accessible Public Grievance Redressal Systems (PGRS), including bureaucratic inertia and lack of resources.
Suggest measures to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen participation in public administration through effective grievance mechanisms and digital platforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Scales of justice overlaid with a digital interface.
GS2.trivia: The Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) is a common platform for citizens to lodge grievances against government organizations.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Combat Malnutrition in Tribal Regions
GS2.explainer:
• Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Health announce a joint initiative.
• Focuses on improving nutritional outcomes for women and children in remote tribal areas.
• Includes provision of fortified food, health check-ups, and awareness campaigns.
• Leverages local community health workers (ASHAs, Anganwadi) for last-mile delivery.
• Aims to address the specific dietary and health challenges faced by tribal populations.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Malnutrition, including stunting, wasting, and anemia, is a significant public health issue in India.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) is the government's flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.
Tribal populations often face higher rates of malnutrition due to geographical isolation, food insecurity, and limited access to healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges, including socio-economic, environmental, and cultural factors, that contribute to malnutrition in tribal communities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government interventions and welfare schemes in addressing tribal health disparities and nutritional deficiencies.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and community-led approaches for sustainable nutritional security and improved health outcomes in these regions.
GS2.imageDescription: A community health worker interacting with tribal women and children.
GS2.trivia: India has the largest tribal population in the world, with over 700 recognized Scheduled Tribes.

GS2.headline: India and France Conduct Joint Naval Exercise in Arabian Sea
GS2.explainer:
• Indian Navy and French Navy conclude a three-day bilateral exercise 'Varuna' in the Arabian Sea.
• Focus on interoperability, maritime security operations, and anti-submarine warfare.
• Involved frontline warships, submarines, and maritime patrol aircraft from both navies.
• Underscores growing strategic partnership and cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Aims to enhance mutual understanding and operational capabilities against common threats.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: 'Varuna' is a prominent bilateral naval exercise between India and France.
France is a key strategic partner for India, particularly in defense, space, and civil nuclear energy sectors.
The Arabian Sea is part of the Indian Ocean, which is a critical maritime trade route and strategically important region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-France defense cooperation in the context of Indo-Pacific security and counterbalancing regional challenges.
Discuss how joint naval exercises like 'Varuna' enhance maritime domain awareness, interoperability, and regional stability.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in bilateral defense partnerships, including technology transfer, joint development, and regional security architectures.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and French warships sailing in formation at sea.
GS2.trivia: India and France also conduct joint air exercise 'Garuda' and joint army exercise 'Shakti'.

GS2.headline: Centre-State Dialogue on Fiscal Decentralization for Local Bodies
GS2.explainer:
• Ministry of Finance convenes a meeting with state representatives to discuss fiscal transfers to Panchayats and Municipalities.
• Focus on strengthening the financial autonomy of local self-governments.
• Deliberations on recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission regarding local body grants.
• Aims to ensure effective devolution of funds for grassroots development.
• Emphasizes the need for robust accounting and auditing mechanisms at the local level.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Finance Commission
prelimsPoints: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments institutionalized Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India.
The Finance Commission (Article 280) recommends the distribution of taxes between the Union and States, and grants-in-aid to states.
State Finance Commissions recommend the principles for the distribution of resources to local bodies within a state.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by local self-governments in India regarding financial autonomy and their impact on grassroots development.
Analyze the role of the Union Finance Commission in strengthening fiscal federalism and promoting devolution of funds to the local level.
Suggest measures to enhance the financial viability, accountability, and capacity building of Panchayats and Municipalities for effective governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Diagram illustrating flow of funds from central government to states and local bodies.
GS2.trivia: The 16th Finance Commission was constituted in December 2023 and is expected to submit its report by October 2025.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision 2047' Blueprint for Developed India, Focus on Green Growth & Digital Economy
GS3.explainer:
• Prime Minister's Office releases comprehensive framework for 'Viksit Bharat @2047'.
• Emphasizes sustainable development, technological innovation, and inclusive growth.
• Key pillars include green energy transition, digital public infrastructure expansion, and human capital development.
• Calls for collaborative efforts from states, private sector, and civil society.
• Aims to position India as a global economic powerhouse by its centenary of independence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047 aims for India to be a developed nation by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
Focus areas include green growth, digital economy, human capital development.
The vision is based on principles of 'Panch Pran' enunciated by the Prime Minister.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in achieving Viksit Bharat @2047 targets, including social inequalities, environmental concerns, and geopolitical uncertainties.
Analyze the crucial role of green growth and digital public infrastructure (DPI) in fostering sustainable and inclusive development.
Evaluate the necessity of a multi-stakeholder approach, involving central and state governments, private sector, and civil society, for realizing the national vision.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic showing 'Viksit Bharat @2047' logo with elements of green energy and digital connectivity.
GS3.trivia: India aims to be a developed nation by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.

GS3.headline: RBI Raises Concerns Over Global Economic Slowdown, Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate
GS3.explainer:
• Reserve Bank of India's latest monetary policy review highlights risks from global slowdown and geopolitical tensions.
• Cites persistent inflationary pressures, albeit moderating, as a key factor in policy stance.
• Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decides to keep the repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive time.
• Focus remains on aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Emphasizes vigilance on liquidity management and financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC sets the repo rate, which is the rate at which commercial banks borrow money from the RBI.
The current inflation target set by the government for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
The MPC comprises six members, three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and growth promotion in a volatile global economic environment.
Discuss the impact of a global economic slowdown and geopolitical tensions on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools, such as the repo rate and liquidity management, in achieving their objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI building with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members, three from RBI and three appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: New National Clean Air Programme Targets for 2030 Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announces revised targets for NCAP.
• Aims for 50-60% reduction in particulate matter concentration in 131 non-attainment cities by 2030.
• Focus on source apportionment studies, real-time monitoring, and technological interventions.
• Enhanced funding and inter-ministerial coordination for effective implementation.
• Emphasizes public awareness and behavioral change for better air quality management.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) was launched in 2019.
The original target of NCAP was to achieve a 20-30% reduction in PM concentration by 2024.
Non-attainment cities are those that consistently fail to meet national ambient air quality standards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of NCAP in improving urban air quality since its inception and the reasons for revising targets.
Discuss the socio-economic and health impacts of air pollution in India, particularly on vulnerable populations.
Suggest innovative and sustainable solutions for achieving ambitious clean air targets, including technological, policy, and behavioral changes.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of Delhi skyline with reduced smog, symbolizing cleaner air.
GS3.trivia: According to WHO, air pollution is one of the biggest environmental threats to human health.

GS3.headline: ISRO Prepares for Venus Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' Launch in Early 2025
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) confirms readiness for its maiden mission to Venus.
• Shukrayaan-1 will study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface using various instruments.
• Mission aims to understand the planet's geological evolution and atmospheric composition.
• Collaboration with international partners for some payloads and data analysis.
• Represents India's expanding capabilities in interplanetary exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Shukrayaan-1 is India's first dedicated mission to Venus.
Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to its similar size and mass.
ISRO's previous interplanetary missions include Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) and Chandrayaan series to the Moon.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's planetary exploration missions in enhancing its scientific prowess and technological capabilities.
Analyze the scientific objectives and potential discoveries of Shukrayaan-1, particularly regarding Venus's extreme environment and geological activity.
Evaluate the role of international collaboration in space missions and how it benefits India's space program.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 orbiter around Venus.
GS3.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in our solar system, meaning the sun rises in the west and sets in the east.

GS3.headline: India's Logistics Performance Index (LPI) Shows Improvement, Challenges Remain
GS3.explainer:
• World Bank's latest LPI report indicates India's continued progress in logistics infrastructure and services.
• Improvements noted in customs efficiency, infrastructure quality, and international shipments.
• Digitalization of logistics processes and multi-modal connectivity projects contribute to the rise.
• Challenges persist in cost efficiency, lead times, and achieving last-mile connectivity.
• Government's National Logistics Policy (NLP) aims to further streamline the sector.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: The Logistics Performance Index (LPI) is an interactive benchmarking tool created by the World Bank.
It evaluates six key dimensions of logistics performance: customs, infrastructure, international shipments, logistics competence, tracking & tracing, and timeliness.
The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan aims for integrated planning and synchronized implementation of infrastructure projects.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of a robust and efficient logistics sector for India's economic growth, global competitiveness, and 'Make in India' initiative.
Discuss the key initiatives undertaken by the government under the National Logistics Policy (NLP) to streamline the sector and reduce logistics costs.
Identify the remaining challenges in improving India's LPI ranking, particularly in areas like cost efficiency, lead times, and last-mile connectivity.
GS3.imageDescription: Container ships at a port with trucks and trains, symbolizing multi-modal logistics.
GS3.trivia: India aims to reduce the cost of logistics as a percentage of GDP from 13-14% to 8% by 2030.

GS3.headline: Experts Call for Stronger Measures to Protect Western Ghats Biodiversity
GS3.explainer:
• Environmentalists and scientists highlight escalating threats to the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Concerns raised over unchecked development, deforestation, and climate change impacts.
• Advocate for stricter implementation of environmental regulations and buffer zone protection.
• Emphasize the unique ecological significance of the region as a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Calls for integrated management plans involving all six states sharing the Ghats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and its conservation
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and are spread across six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
The Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees provided recommendations for the conservation and protection of the Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats, including its role as a water tower and repository of biodiversity, and the various threats it currently faces.
Analyze the recommendations of expert committees like Gadgil and Kasturirangan for Western Ghats conservation and the challenges in their implementation.
Propose a sustainable development model for the region that balances conservation needs with the livelihood requirements of local communities.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a waterfall.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: India Develops Indigenous AI Tool for Early Detection of Oral Cancer
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at IIT Delhi, in collaboration with AIIMS, unveil a groundbreaking AI-powered diagnostic tool.
• Utilizes machine learning algorithms to analyze medical images for early signs of oral cancer.
• Aims to improve diagnosis accuracy and reduce diagnostic delays, especially in rural areas.
• Undergoing clinical trials and seeking regulatory approvals for widespread use.
• Highlights India's growing prowess in applying AI for public health challenges.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science focused on creating intelligent machines.
Oral cancer is a major health concern in India, often linked to tobacco use, and early detection significantly improves treatment outcomes.
Indigenous development of AI tools contributes to self-reliance in critical sectors like healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing healthcare delivery, diagnostics, and treatment in India.
Analyze the ethical considerations, data privacy concerns, and challenges in deploying AI-based diagnostic tools in a diverse healthcare landscape.
Evaluate how indigenous AI solutions can effectively address specific Indian health challenges and contribute to equitable access to healthcare.
GS3.imageDescription: Medical professional using a tablet with an AI interface to analyze oral scans.
GS3.trivia: India accounts for nearly one-third of the global burden of oral cancer.

GS3.headline: Enhanced Focus on Coastal Security after Recent Incidents
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Coast Guard and Navy intensify surveillance along coastal areas following intelligence inputs.
• Review of existing coastal security framework involving state police and customs.
• Emphasis on integrating technology, including radar systems and drones, for real-time monitoring.
• Training and capacity building for local fishing communities as 'eyes and ears' of security agencies.
• Aims to prevent infiltration, smuggling, and other illicit activities from the sea.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is responsible for maritime law enforcement and coastal security in India's territorial waters.
The 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks highlighted significant vulnerabilities in India's coastal security framework.
The Multi-Agency Centre (MAC) serves as a common platform for intelligence sharing among various security agencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional threats, including terrorism, smuggling, and illegal migration, to India's extensive coastline.
Analyze the measures taken by the government to strengthen coastal defense mechanisms and enhance maritime domain awareness since major security incidents.
Evaluate the role of technology, inter-agency coordination, and community participation in ensuring comprehensive coastal security.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard ship patrolling the coastline.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including island territories, making coastal security a complex challenge.

GS3.headline: Government Promotes Climate-Resilient Agriculture Through Digital Tools
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of Agriculture launches new initiatives to integrate digital technologies for climate-smart farming.
• Focus on precision agriculture, weather advisories, and market linkages through mobile apps.
• Aims to help farmers adapt to changing climate patterns and enhance crop productivity.
• Provides subsidies for adoption of climate-resilient crop varieties and water-efficient irrigation.
• Part of broader efforts to double farmers' income and ensure food security.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: Climate-resilient agriculture aims to reduce the vulnerability of agriculture to the adverse impacts of climate change.
Digital tools like remote sensing, AI, and IoT are being increasingly used for precision farming and providing real-time weather advisories.
India's agriculture sector is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts, including erratic rainfall and extreme weather events.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and farmers' livelihoods.
Analyze how digital technologies and precision farming can enhance climate resilience, optimize resource utilization, and improve productivity in the agricultural sector.
Evaluate the policy interventions and support mechanisms required to promote sustainable and climate-smart agriculture among small and marginal farmers.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer using a smartphone in a field with modern irrigation systems.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top agricultural producers globally, but over 50% of its cultivable land is rainfed, making it susceptible to monsoon variations.
